summary.scope_note: Per close.md AC-2.6 cleanliness scope, only violations newly introduced by this cycle's diff are in scope. The two changed files are pre-existing functional hooks; the diff added /do consent-flag + /allow grant bypass logic and realigned LINE_LIMIT (1000->600 in read-size-guard). No new files, no temp/backup residue, no dead code, no orphan imports, no unreferenced helpers introduced. Triple python3 -c spawn in the bash hook is a stylistic/perf concern outside the 17-category cleanliness catalog (not flagged).
diff_categories_reviewed: temp_files (verified absent in hooks/ via mtime+pattern scan)
unreferenced_scripts (no new scripts; lib/allowlist.read_grant + new has_consent() helper both used at line 72 of read-size-guard)
obsolete_functionality (none - bypass branches add behavior, do not deprecate any path)
dead_functions (verified: has_consent fn used line 72; no orphan top-level defs)

file_a.diff_summary: Added sys.path.insert + from lib.allowlist import read_grant (lines 13-16). Added has_consent() helper (lines 25-30). Added main-agent bypass branch (lines 69-75). Changed LINE_LIMIT from 1000 to 600 (line 21). All new imports and helpers are consumed within the same file.
file_a.cleanliness_check: PASS — no temp residue, no dead code, three-way alignment with docstring + CLAUDE.md achieved.

file_b.diff_summary: Converted env-var TOOL_NAME read to JSON-from-stdin parse (lines 13-19). Added main-agent-only /do consent flag bypass (lines 26-39). Added /allow EnterWorktree grant bypass via heredoc invocation of lib/allowlist.read_grant (lines 40-55). Original block message + exit 2 preserved (lines 58-67).
file_b.cleanliness_check: PASS — bash hook uses no temp files, no dead code. Three python3 -c invocations for JSON parsing are stylistic/perf opportunity, not cleanliness-catalog violations.
